zh.Stafford, Staffordshire, England: 英國斯塔福爾斯塔福德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan De STAFFORD於1269年出生時，她的父親Robert de Stafford53歲，母親Alice de Corbet44歲。她和Sir John de Chetwynd至少有1個兒子。她去世於大約1292年在英國斯塔福爾斯塔福德享年24歲。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: STAFFORD
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name principally from Stafford (Staffordshire), but occasionally from other places with similar names, such as Stafford House in Ifield (Sussex), possibly East and West Stowford in East Down (Devon), and three minor places in Devon called Stafford, in Dolton, Broadhembury, and Colyton parishes. The places are all named with Old English ford ‘ford’ as the final element, but have different initial elements. The Staffordshire placename has Old English stæth ‘river bank, shore’; Stafford in Colyton (Devon) has Old English stān ‘stone, rock’; the Sussex placename has Old English stēor ‘steer, bullock’; East and West Stowford (Devon) have Old English stæf ‘staff, stave, rod’; Stafford in Dolton and Stafford in Broadhembury (Devon) may have Old English stæth, stān, or stæf.

variants.name: Stocker
meanings: German and Swiss German (also Stöcker): topographic name for someone living by a tree stump (see Stock 3) or an occupational name for a tree cutter, from Middle High German stocken ‘to clear land’.
German and Swiss German (also Stöcker); Dutch: occupational name from Middle High German, Middle Dutch stocker ‘jailer’.

English: occupational name from Middle English stokker ‘one who sells stockfish’ (fish dried in the air without salt). This was the usual source of the name in medieval London, where a bylaw of 1419 stated that no stokker should board a ship to buy fish (presumably in order to forestall the market).

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan De STAFFORD was born in 1269, her father, Robert de Stafford, was 53 and her mother, Alice de Corbet, was 44. She had at least 1 son with Sir John de Chetwynd. She died about 1292, in Stafford, Staffordshire, England, at the age of 24.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan De STAFFORD est née en 1269, son père, Robert de Stafford, avait 53 ans et sa mère, Alice de Corbet, avait 44 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Sir John de Chetwynd. Elle est décédée environ 1292, à Stafford, Staffordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 24 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan De STAFFORD 1269 geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert de Stafford 53 und ihre Mutter Alice de Corbet 44. Sie hatte mit Sir John de Chetwynd mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb um 1292 im Alter von 24 Jahren in Stafford, Staffordshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan De STAFFORD è nata il nel 1269, sua padre, Robert de Stafford, aveva 53 anni e sua madre, Alice de Corbet, aveva 44 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Sir John de Chetwynd. Morì circa 1292, nel Stafford, Staffordshire, England, all'età di 24 anni.

ja.Chetwynd, Shropshire, England: イギリスシュロプシュアイアチェトウインド
ja.lifeSummary: Joan De STAFFORDが1269年に生まれた時，父Robert de Staffordは53歳，母Alice de Corbetは44歳であった。彼女はSir John de Chetwyndとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は約1292年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアスタフォードで24歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan De STAFFORD(이)가 1269년에 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert de Stafford(은)는 53세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice de Corbet(은)는 44세였다. 그녀는 Sir John de Chetwynd(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1292년 영국 스태퍼드실르 스태퍼드에서 24세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan De STAFFORD nasceu em 1269, seu pai, Robert de Stafford, tinha 53 anos e sua mãe, Alice de Corbet, tinha 44 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Sir John de Chetwynd. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1292, em Stafford, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, com 24 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan De STAFFORD родилась в 1269 г., её отцу по имени Robert de Stafford было 53 года, а матери, которую звали Alice de Corbet, 44. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Sir John de Chetwynd. Она умерла около 1292 г. в местности стаффорд, стаффордшир, Англия в возрасте 24 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan De STAFFORD nació en 1269, su papá, Robert de Stafford, tenía 53 años y su mamá, Alice de Corbet, tenía 44 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Sir John de Chetwynd. Murió aproximadamente 1292, en Stafford, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 24 años.
